channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 49 replies — 145.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-05-30 05:42 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287362
>>62286450
moar

>>62286297
same, I don't like to think about it.

62289019
>2026
>BAT
wew

62289108
>>62286063
>>62286122
jolly good jiggling jugs TP
>>62286450
seconding moar of this btggf or even her username

62289174
How is this board still discussing dead shitcoins from 10 years ago
Does no one here like making money

62289182
>>62287337
lmao she do be living high off the BAT scam

62289202
>>62289174
BAT threads are just places for us to lament and to talk to the friends we met along the way

62289359
>>62286122
what the fuck is this whore's name

62289403
>>62286122
>gnarly memory leaks
no its working damn fine on my machine
I had to fix youtube by element blocking the share links though

62289404
>>62286122
Man

62289650
>>62287337
The fuck you doing? We were supposed to keep this from the peasants

62290320
>>62287337
you mean her name isn't even jennie? wtf scammed

62290344
>>62289108
Hey Lad! Long time, no see. I'll switch PCs in a bit and see if I can find the profile for you.

>>62289359
Give me a bit and I'll see what I can do for you. I have like 8 more from that set and some other ones in the meantime I can post.

>>62289403
I'm getting like 5GB+ memory usage from a single YouTube tab. Tends to only happen on livestreams or really long videos. Let me know if you have a request!

>>62290122
Are you requesting feet?

62290355
>>62287337
That's so foul, blowing up her page like that. She better private that shit ASAP.

>>62287362
I'm working on it, but here's another thicc goth for (You).

62290540
>>62290344
>I'm getting like 5GB+ memory usage from a single YouTube tab]
its a known bug yt's end, the like and share (etc) interface below the video is caught in an endless live loop
to fix it do this: go to settings then find shields then select content filtering then scroll down to create custom filters and paste this in the box field www.youtube.com###menu
then save changes
let me know how you get on

62290561
>>62290344
>I'll switch PCs in a bit and see if I can find the profile for you.
thanks TP, you're the best of 'em
>Hey Lad! Long time, no see
I'm working abroad again
trying to get settled in
what about you? doing well I hope

62290662
>>62290540
I really appreciate that, lad! Thanks a lot! I'll give it a go in a bit.

>>62290561
>you're the best of 'em
Back at you!
>I'm working abroad again
Where do they have you this time?

62290702
>>62290662
yw TP, that fix is a life saver, but if you're still having issues then tell me as I have a few more filters that will help
back in kong of the hong variety for as long as needed
hot damn that is one holy soft thicc and wobbly thot

62290728
>>62290344
this bitch is just ridiculous

62290732
>>62290662
HOLY FUCK LORD HAVE MERCY

62290812
>>62290702
Seems to be doing a lot better. I really appreciate it!
>HK
sick! I might be going to Shanghai sometime later in the Summer. If I make it out there, I'll let you know. Hope you have a comfy stay!

>>62290728
>>62290732
I got the name for you >>62286122
Cece Rose. I'll dump more of the OF if you don't want to pay for it.

_
Might get a vacay for this one, but fuck it.

62290849
>>62290812
cool TP
yeah let me know
this apartment is way better than the last place I was assigned thank fuck
kekeke we'll see you next thread ig but worth it lel
jannies might have chilled tho I hope

62290910
>>62290812
LEGEND!

I'm gonna need the name of this goth whore next. Are those braces?

62291155
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
Fucking hell, TP. More of this please, especially >>62290355

62291188
>>62285628
Nope. Sold everything when it became clear they’re not actually interested in building utility for the token. It’s a memecoin connected to a browser. They had the world by the balls and they made a conscious decision to do absolutely nothing for BAT. They cannot be trusted

62291222
>>62290355
You're a pornographer, the fuck you care?

62291254
>>62286450
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
TP MOGS /gif/
THE MOTHERFUCKING GOAT

62291656
>>62290812
Wonderful rack on her. Lets see some asian MILFS GILFS

62291660
>>62287337
she's hands down the hottest crypto girl, the hottest thing about her is that she hides how big her ass is, won't even show it off out of modesty

62291667
>>62289174
Most BAT holders moved on to something else eventually, but still hold some BAT out of loyalty and the purest copium you'll ever find

Anyone who was "all-in" on BAT has sold or killed themselves by now

item.title: Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99 LMAO HAHA… — 35 replies — 140.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99 LMAO HAHA…

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-05-30 06:02 UTC

OP

Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99

LMAO HAHAHAHAHAHA OH NO NO LINKIES OH NO NO

Early replies (first 3)

62287876
>>62287844
we aren't cuckolds, okay? We just like knowing that Sergey keeps getting richer while Chainlink is stuck at 2020 prices

62288485
kek stinkies

62288499
>>62287844
xlm always does this before the bull..

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62288748
>>62287844
Well XLM pumps 40% and on same even bigger news LINK dumps 9%

Other asset is suppressed and another isnt

62288856
>>62288748
>Other asset is suppressed and another isnt

KABOOOOM! So bullish

62288910
>>62288539
It makes no sense. The SF is paying chainlink to be a part of SCALE. Theyre paying for this integration with the DTCC.

62288931
>>62288910
>chainlink

62288957
>>62288910
Remember when department of commerce announcement happened and wormhole pumped 20% while LINK dumped 5%

Its basically repeat of that, demoralization & suppression

Big wallets keep accumulating and stacklets keep selling

62289062
>>62288957
That just means the pumps are meaningless and based on nothing, just pure speculation by the people controlling the token.

62289072
>>62288957
>Big wallets keep accumulating

62289486
>>62288910
>cryptos are paying for the integration with DTCC
>my special crypto does not tho, that's legit
???

62289511
>>62289072
Yeah all those metrics are fake goy, do not buy LINK

62289523
>>62289511
>teh metrics
what metrics bruh?

62289559
>>62287844
link's time is coming

62289566
>>62289511
>SAAAAAAAAAR BIG WALLETS KEEP BUYING SAAAAR WHALES ARE ACCUMULATING

62289658
DTCC keep repeating over and over that they're going to use all kinds of chains. Stellar is just one of them.
Meanwhile Chainlink has a quasi monopoly.

yet this is how the market reacts.
No wonder crypto is getting is ass beat by the stock market so badly.

62289736
>>62289658
>saaar the market wrong saaar chainlink very valuable, note it

62289745
>>62289658
claiming that "they're going to use all kinds of chains" inherently implies that they're going to use the corresponding blockchain tokens as well, since you know, that's required when interacting with each chain
having said that, please point to me one instance of DTCC stating that they're using the LINK token

62289756
>>62289745
>please point to me one instance of DTCC stating that they're using the LINK token
The Chainlink products they're using need the Link token.

62289763
>>62289756
>can't even bring up a single instance

62289813
>>62289763
Instance of what?

62289925
>>62287844
I don't care about your (((compliant))) coin

62290228
>>62287844
I started the link cuckold meme. KNEEL

62290247
>>62289745
Chainlink will literally abstract away any gas token

One token all chains (the one and only LINK)

62290559
>>62287844
MOM IT'S DOING IT AGAIN

62290596
>>62289745
kek see this >>62290247

Not only are they going to be buying Link tokens, but they'll be buying Link tokens in order to pay for the gas on the corresponding blockchain. Because the fee in Link takes care of the gas.

62290754
>>62288539
I’ve fucked two guys wives before and filmed it

Idk what possesses dudes to be into this kind of shit but I got paid so

62290965
Xlm will actually hit $1

62291397
>xlm +43%
Damn linkies are fucking cooked.. when's the last time link had a pump like that? 2019??

62291419
>xlm up 100%
Kek linkies

62291508
>Almost in the top #10

Kek stinkies wish they had this kind of performance

62291680
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOm

62291689
>>62287844
Wow XLM sure is running! It must be so much higher than it was a year ago, right?????

item.title: Saar! Buy my Hyderabad. Saar, DO NOT Redeem! Bloody Bastard Bitch! — 21 replies — 54.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saar! Buy my Hyderabad. Saar, DO NOT Redeem! Bloody Bastard Bitch!

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-30 05:39 UTC

OP

Saar! Buy my Hyderabad. Saar, DO NOT Redeem!
Bloody Bastard Bitch!

Early replies (first 3)

62290396
>>62290392
Kek
Will fade soon

62290399
revenue only go up, no competitors sir very good token.

62290406
>>62290399
>no competitors
>edgex appears behind you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290415
>>62290406
sir do not fud please. many moons ahead

62290421
>>62290399
>muh revenue

This shit will have heaviest baggiest in crypto soon

62290426
>>62290392
60b fdv
22% circulating

62290428
>>62290392
This, so much this. Only jeets hold the best-performing crypto of 2025 and 2026.
True aryans hold russian scams like Chainlink and Ethereum.

62290432
>>62290392
>>62290396
>>62290399
>>62290406
>>62290415
>>62290421
>>62290426

62290458
>>62290432
past performance is not indicative of future results

62290466
>>62290432
mentally ill tranny, my post had nothing to do with link

62290474
>>62290466
No, you're somehow even more pathetic chasing a bucket shop with washed metrics as a purely cope play because you missed out on the most tokenholder-aligned project since BTC.

62290484
>>62290474
banks will use edgex

62290506
>>62290432
Nobody gives a flying fuck about your shitcoin saar, pls go and buy the space ipo with the good goyim!

62290530
>>62290506
If you don't care then why coordinate this FUD spam anytime Hype is mentioned and not any of the other goyraped alts regularly posted here? I've never hard shilled Hype here once because I legitimately don't give a fuck about this place anymore

62290534
>>62290530
I am saaring in every saar shitcoin not just this one.

62290542
>>62290534
Fair, I'll stop talking about my bags and just join in on the link FUD

62290547
Saaaarses lets not waste more izzat and settle this in the traditional way. We all go to the ganges tomorrow and the one who drinks more holy water will win.

62290588
Thanks for confirming this is this cycle's sol

62290698
>>62290432
why do linkfags and xrpfags hate hype anyway.

62290701
>>62290542
Its a hyderabad fud thread, did you lost arsh goyal saar?

62291657
Jewish man who runs the NYSE says he is deeply invested in HYPE so he can get even more money from goyim, he is also a close friend of Donald "top goy" Trump.

item.title: I made a drink that was revealed to me in a dream. How do I sell it? — 17 replies — 48.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I made a drink that was revealed to me in a dream. How do I sell it?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-30 05:42 UTC

OP

It's one of the most delicious things I've ever had. Genuinely top 10 drinks. I wanna sell it, but... How? Who do I pitch this to? How tf do you market "this recipe was revealed to me in a dream?" Everyone who's tried it loves it but IDK where to go from here.

Early replies (first 3)

62290943
>>62290941
moon brew...

62290953
>>62290941
go to pepsico
walk in the front door
find the owner
stand up straight, look him in the eye, give him a firm handshake
tell him you had a dream about a drink and pepsis and money
????
profit

62290960
give me the recipe and i'll tell you if it's actually good. i have god tier taste buds and taste in general. . Normies fear my eloquent opinions.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290979
>>62290960
I'm absolutely not giving up the recipe. Dream your own drinks.
>>62290953
It's gotta be firm or it doesn't work

62291614
If it has to be made fresh? Form an LLC, save up enough money to rent out a small store or street facing bar/stand whatever with signage out front etc. and make it known locally. If you are truly destitute poor then set up a table at all the local farmers markets in every town within 5 hours of you and sell it there. Hire hot women to sell it at these stands/markets/stores once you've saved enough money then get it to start trending on social media short form videos hiring hot girls and guys to drink it in videos so people will travel there to try it

62291617
>>62291614
if it's any good it would take professional tasters about 3 minutes to figure out exactly how to copy it and set up competing businesses.

that's the problem with food in the US. You can't actually stop rivals from copying your recipe

62291622
>>62291617
add some natural food coloring to it to throw them off for a few more years. Doesn't need to be blue or something just make it the wrong neutral natural looking color. OP i want to taste your delicious drink

62291625
>>62291622
lol

changing the color isn't going to throw off anyone that tastes food for a living

and that just makes it easier to copy
>OP sues competitor for copying his drink
>but yours is blue, ours isn't!
>lawsuit gets tossed
>OP dies in a back alley for lack of a working business model

62291633
>>62291625
this is like saying you should never start a business because there are other businesses out there so don't try. There aren't going to be professional taste testers at the farmers market trying to set up another beverage table lmao. Even if there were, it will take years for a major business to roll out their copy to the masses going through all the legal trouble and logistics and figuring out how to get it shelf stable in bottle and OP will have already made good money off it by then just like what happens to every good new product

62291638
>>62291633
I'm talking about the point where he's renting buildings and setting up new locations

at that point mcdonalds starbucks and about 5000 other bars and restaurants are going to send tasters out and in a matter of weeks will have sourced bulk ingredients, safety and marketing tested the drinks, and implemented them across thousands of existing stores

>how to get it shelf stable in bottle
that's not how restaurants and bars in the US sell things.

62291641
>>62291638
Yeah I can see that happening but that will take time, he still has time to make money from it before then, and if he wants he still has a decent shot at marketing his street facing walk up bar as having "the original moon brew" or whatever and can still make some money. What I'm saying is that he should still try so that I can taste this thing

62291647
>>62291641
Yes, it can be done. He's probably going to have to sell to a competitor eventually though I'd guess. Or take on investors to get the capital to go big.

I'm lazy, I'd just take the recipe directly to someone that already has the marketing and locations to produce it in place right off the bat.

62291649
>>62291647
those are all good ideas and would make money from a dream which is pretty funny, i'm rooting for you OP it better not be chocolate milk with saffron added or some shit

62291651
>>62291649
>it better not be chocolate milk with saffron added or some shit
kek'd out loud

I bet it is.

>Orange juice with vanilla, milk, and an egg made billions

62291662
>>62290941
you dont unless your dream told you how to sell it.

you will fail

62291664
>>62291651
kek now i want an orange julius god this thread makes me thirsty, i hope it's something strange like that

item.title: lever up on XLM now. — 20 replies — 27.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
lever up on XLM now.

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-30 05:20 UTC

OP

this pile of shit is always good for the yearly crime candle and it's due with the DTCC news.
>Only coin in the top 100 green today
>Massive spike in volume on spot and perps (2nd day)
They'll crime this one for a at least a few more days. Time to make your money back anon. Ogs remember the 2021 xlm crime candle that kickstarted the bull cycle.

Early replies (first 3)

62283130
buncha noobs also going to short it cus muh mean reversion everything else is crashing!

62283353
>>62283344

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283356
>>62283117
What is a crime candle?

62283608
>>62283356
It's a politically correct alternative term.

62283617
>>62283356

62287293
>>62283617
That's a bart nig

62287816
https://x.com/Dr_Kritsada/status/2060289369047798078?s=20

62287833
So fxcking bullish xlm

62291090
posted at 17c btw, 5x long to 26c woulda been over a fast 2.5x

62291186
>>62291090
>just 5x long a vertical chart in a bear market bro
lol piss off

62291237
>>62291186
that's what made it so easy, 99.8% of the coins were dying that day and XLM was glowing green with huge volume, you don't fade that type of strength. XLM is a known crime coin, when it moves it moves big for at least a few days. It wasn't just gonna be a 1 day pnd.

62291244
clowns trying to short it cause muh too green in bear market is simply what propels it higher. I was watching the movement on binance perps they were luring people in baiting shorts but maintaining the overall structure before they'd rip it higher.

62291389
>>62291244
Fair play you crazy cat but it wasn't an easy call

62291531
>>62283117
So is this pretty much confirmation that chainlink doesn’t direct value to their token if the same news that made link pump 0% makes this shit coin pump 50%+

62291534
bullish asfxck at xlm

62291537
Holy shit my XLM bags are going to the moon

Imagine being a chainlinkie right now and being left out top kek

62291630
>>62291537
This

item.title: Is HYPE overvalued? — 24 replies — 11.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is HYPE overvalued?

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-30 03:59 UTC

OP

Is HYPE overvalued?

Early replies (first 3)

62289589
>>62289577
No, governance tokens that give you exchange fee discounts are, uh, a revolutionary breakthrough

62289600
>>62289577
>>62289589
Check how the last twitter hype token ended, SUI. Its down 80% and dead. HYPE will end the same.

62289622
It's value is too high relative to the rest of the market, but it's a bit undervalued at the moment for what it should be.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62289657
>>62289600
I mean the buybacks are bullish as hell. This shit jumped today because of SpaceX speculation. Will see similar scenes before Anthropic and OpenAI. Stay poor Bobo. Biz can be handed one of the best projects in a long ass time and still try to slide it.

62289688
>>62289589
Governance token that gets 99% of the revenue bought back and burned. That's 10 figures a year. That IS revolutionary coming from this shithole with guys like Hoskinsin and Richard Heart fleecing you goycattle for their blackhawks

62289695
>>62289577
Yes and no.
95% of perp traders have a negative pnl. They are only profitable because retards think they'll get rich quick instead of just buying spot
Eventually this fad will fade. HYPE only has a future as long as normies can be convinced to dump their paycheque into perps and lose it

62289708
>>62289695
>buy hype as long as you think people will keep gambling

here, I phrased it better.

62289718
>>62289695
Human nature will fade? Lol. Bold bet.

62289726
People still don't even know hyperliquid is a blockchain and hype is the gas token. There are already more dapps than in fucking cardano.

You're still early

62289730
>>62289657
Its a new paradigm

We will all be rich

62289744
>>62289688
checked, don't forget fat sirgay. he literally raped me back in 2018, and I'm continue being raped to this day. I demand raperations

62289758
Hype is going to get regulated out of existence and "they" will create a perps chain that is suitable for goys.

62289762
>>62289744
Uh oh stinky

62289791
on one hand, it has very little moat. many perps dexes have already come and gone, most with tokens that had some kind of revenue share. on the other hand, it has persisted against tough pressure from binance without folding, and its revenue is among the strongest across defi protocols.

62289800
>>62289577
Already a Unicorn bro

62289842
>>62289589
yeah thats what i thought and then BNB pumped to 100B+. Im not missing this one, faggot.

62289859
>>62289762
RAPE
>>62289842
surely it must go down again to 50 at least, right? right???

62289928
>>62289859
no

62290297
>>62289577
what is this FUD all of a sudden? is somebody jelouse?

62290356
>>62289577
No, but soon Aero will be the new Hype.

62290405
>>62290356
Nah, Nerva will be the new one. It can ONLY be mined via CPU and it's anonymous like crypto was supposed to be. Still a small MC so it has tons of growth potential. I haven't found any others like it yet, as anything similar has already had their growth spurt, but would love if anyone knows of some to diversify!

62290725
>token appears to be the breakout top 5 of the next cycle
>will likely hit 450b mcap, be just behind btc, eth, bnb and sol (I don't care about stablecoins)
>currently cheap around 15b
>/biz/GENIUSES absolutely fucking hate it, probably because it uses its own oracles and doesn't rely on link and we have a thousand baggies on this board who refuse to make money because a fat man shilled them a scam once almost 10 years ago
>will likely have copycats like aster that will be 'hype killers' that will do okay but ultimately fail and die
To my infinite regert, I should have full ported this shit in feb/march when it was only 22 bucks.

62291375
>>62289695
I've learned more often than not, you should not fade projects that appeal to gambling addicts.

62291481
HYPE has a bright future. My only regret is not getting into it in the ground floor. Arthur had told me to make sure to buy some but then I forgot about it. HyperLiquid took too long to release the token for general public and did it without much fanfare.

item.title: I have a feeling ETH will rise back — 16 replies — 11.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have a feeling ETH will rise back

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-30 04:40 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62286999
Standard chartered says ETH to hit $40k

62287001
>>62286999
4.8 trillion market cap? Really nigga?

62287010
>>62286995
From $2.5k down to $1.5k over and over again and you’re still broke. How?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287015
>>62286999
>>62287001
since bitcoin is "digital gold" I assume they just looked up the market cap of silver to make this prediction

62287019
>>62287001
>Distributed computation and storage with active developments in homomorphic encryption is not worth 4.8 trillions dollars.
Why are there so many retards here?

62287186
>>62286995
cope

62288056
>>62286995
I told you to sell in august. Not that i did sell all but a lot.

62288098
>>62286999
Standard Chartered just made up a bunch of numbers and said "this is our forecast" lol

Also checked

62288128
>>62287019
>homomorphic encryption
sounds gay as fuck, no wonder your shitcoin is trading at the same price as 2021

62288202
>>62288128
It's closed source computation.
It's current value is reflective of the ignorance of the network's capabilities.
Can you explain how the Ethereum network works? Save your fingers. You've already demonstrated that you can't.

62290487
>>62286999
I fucking wish it even hits 10K by then, I would retire from the passive income.

62290501
waiting to get back in. 500 seems a fair entry!!!!

62290515
>>62290501
n-no! it will never fall that low!!! you will buy it for $5000 from me and I will be laughing at you!

62290783
>>62287001
Eth has x10 more utility then bitcoin so it should be $14 trillion right now. I bought.

62291364
>>62286995
uni v4 hooks will bring a new wave of shitcoin revivals. it's the perfect thing to do a cto with

62291558
>>62286995
I’m bullish on ETH the network but bearish as fuck on ETH the token. There’s better scarce hard money assets on the eth network that don’t dump for 5 years straight under immense sell pressure from the team

item.title: Rescue your dying value — 5 replies — 10.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Rescue your dying value

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-30 05:34 UTC

OP

Swap them for XMR. Purposeless coins are going to 0 by 2028

Early replies (first 3)

62291057
>>62290990
I hope someone posts a cut off graph showing the recent fall in price just so another person can post how quickly we recovered.

62291081
>>62290990
I definitely understand the case for XMR, but ETH now has heavy institutional buying and they're moving stocks/rwa on chain. It's pretty clear if you are watching where elites are pivoting towards in crypto that it's ETH. It's all scripted, guaranteed that Ethereum will tenbag by 2028, all the Jews are paying Congress and they'll pass clarity act by surprise while the public isn't paying attention. BTC is objectively going to get raped by quantum computing, not as an if, but a when.

62291217
>>62291081
No shade to ETH specifically

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62291302
Larry Fink himself said the future they are building is a decentralized banking system for the consumers around the world.

So the future will rely on blockchain and tokens that facilitate this like XRP.

https://youtu.be/5urrOWO0KDI

62291650
>>62290990
Saaar?

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 140 replies — 19.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 140 • Updated: 2026-05-29 23:00 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283926
>>62283901
diminishing returns mean diminishing drawbacks, retard

62283940
>>62282847
I hope that big brain stuck around, for my sake

62283952
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a pyramid scheme and a waste of electricity. I was an early adopter of bitcoin fully thinking it was a scam and I sold mine in 2016 after I had enough to retire. I understand the whole point about fiat currency but what I think we need to do is just get rid of money all together and use assets as money like stocks and commodities. Money was invented so people didn't have to carry their assets around but know we all walk around with computers in our pocket that would allow us to trade our assets like how things used to be. I should be able to go to McDonalds and buy my food with corn for example. Bitcoin only exists for countries to bypass sanctions and if you buy bitcoin you are just giving more money to Putin and all the other scammers and terrorists.

62285517
>>62272225
They've got their preferences

62285578
>>62283375
Yeah, DeFi hacks are getting pretty crazy lately. That’s why multiple audits, formal verification, penetration testing, and cryptography reviews matter much more now with this much capital involved.

62285585
>>62272647
You'll regret your choices lol

62285589
>>62272667
You think you're smart?

62286335
>>62271712
No money, no job
They literally won't even take me at minimum wage places because I don't speak spanish.
So, no way to generate the income to buy BTC in the first place.

62287162
>>62274011
Work for it anon

62287177
>>62283952
You can already by McDonalds with corn. Use credit card and pay off credit card with corn futures. The problem is your dumbass is bleeding wealth by keeping your net worth in corn (or companies, or anything besides bitcoin) because people are going to make more corn (and ever faster each year due to tech advances) and inflate away your wealth.

62287383
>>62285578
but why remains the hack

62287392
>>62286335
If you're married at don't have money, you deserve a divorce

62287393
>>62271712
It's a gamble and I only bet on sure things. In fact I like to have my profits guaranteed under contract and insured BEFORE I spend a dime of my own money. I always sell BEFORE I buy so I know exactly how much I'm making in advance.

62287464
Most of the retards have been bled dry. BTC only exists now as a system for whales to lead exit pumps and bleed the occasional new entrants.

62287628
>>62287383
Most of the biggest DeFi disasters over the years involved bridges, custodians, or avoidable trust assumptions somewhere in the stack. That's exactly why security architecture gets attention now.

62287630
>>62280441
You're thinking the wrong way

62287632
>>62282426
Well, depends on them

62287634
BTC has just tracked the nasdaq for years until recently and now it just sucks ass no matter what happens.

Hype has peaked. Most of it is owned by boomers now and its not a hedge against anything it just dumps the hardest on bad news until boomers, banks and third worlders get tired of bagholding and it goes sub 20k forever

62287690
>>62287634
So you're saying btc is only a big hell hype?

62288099
>>62287634
nonsense

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

62288281
>>62271712
Indifference. I got into the market when the BTC was $3K. but I bought Link instead. Pleased with my decision. I respect what it has done and continues to do with the world but I get excited for smart contracts more, which the BTC doesn't have.

62288653
>>62271712
Because I got caught with my pants down avgcost 95k, and I've been trimming it and using that money as play dump money. So far the 15k I sold from Btc and put into Sandisk have turned in 110k already, and the 10k into gold are at 14k, and it's an actual asset. Never buying that retarded shit again. I still have like 30k and maybe I'll keep that, but chances are I'll probably sell and ride the SpaceX pump and dumo with it. Put in 50k into it, sell 50k when it doubles in price, keep the rest as gains. Did the same shit with Micron, Sandisk and silver, take profits and just keep the rest. This is a hype based market and btc has no hype anymore.

62290061
>>62288112
We'll find out when TBVs actually launch.
Then you can judge the product based on what it does, not on what random people online tell you to think.

62290076
>>62275451
Ok, just buy altcoins

62290078
>>62272002
ahahahahahahahah

62290085
>>62275501
If BTC goes to zero, alts are probably even more cooked.

62290480
>>62287464
If it's such an obvious scam, why are you still here watching it?

62290856
>>62271933
There's truth to this, but I dunno about 40k. Buy "dips" now I say

62290931
>>62272803
>>62272874

The conundrum that niggers on here don't understand is that everyone wants upside volatility, but it's the volatility itself that ultimately creates the ceiling.

It's the cute naive chick that ceases to be so once you fuck her. You can't have your cute and fuck it too.

item.title: cardano, ADA is imploding right now — 56 replies — 11.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
cardano, ADA is imploding right now

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-05-30 01:09 UTC

OP

cardano, ADA
is imploding right now

Early replies (first 3)

62275698
the whole space is fucking dying. sad that it all ended up this way

62275723
>>62275591
Charles-sama, why?

62276473
>>62275591
Imagine buying cardanus, ever! This shit has less than 500$ stables liquidity onchain.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62280533
>>62277725
Some cheap twitter beef causing uproar. It's still one of the most technologically sound chains out there, but some people tried a power grab the minute Charles burnt his keys.
So the only mistake that Charles has made there is that he had too much good faith in those that are stabbing him in the back now. I guess that's what you get if you don't know who your real friends are.

62280595
jeets are still buying crypto lmao
lmfao even

62280622
>>62278448
This cope again
Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets” then maybe I’ll change my mind. Until then I think you’re delusional if you think it’s useful or “mass adoption” to have a handful of turbonerd billionaires and hedge funds use USDC or UST in place of traditional clearinghouses

62280637
>>62277444
But muh Haskell

62280714
>>62280622
>Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets”
DTCC is going to use Chainlink for stablecoins too you know.

62280809
>>62278477
It's a pretty good heuristic

62280833
Save what value you can, swap your ADA for XMR and hodl!

62281020
>>62280595
crypto is banned in india
so in a way, you're like a jeet

62281159
I never trusted Charles so I never bought cardano…dude is an obvious psycho

62281608
ENJOY YOUR CARDANO, EARTHMAN!

62281639
>>62275591
>@TheCardanoTimes

62281653
>>62281159
This, one look at that basedboi faggot should tell you all you need to know

If you still made the decision to put money in this project, its your fault, really

62282581
>>62277151
fucking kek charles is such a fucking narcissist

62282732
>>62277151
Wow that's very bold and stupid. Homeboy thought he could just jump into an industry as complex as healthcare. "Mayo Clinic of the West" lmao

62282767
>>62278448
token not needed

62282801
>>62280714
No they aren't.

62282929
>>62282801

62284091
>>62275698
>>62277437
buy signal

62284100
>>62281020
>crypto is banned in india
just googled it and you're full of shit

62284157
>>62277590
>I remember you could play Super Mario 64 on the Internet Computer Protocol (bing bing wahoo on ICP, jej) but who the fuck cares?
The whole point of that experiment killed ICP the moment it was taken off. What's the point of your web infrastructure if regular web rules still apply to it? Why the fuck is your product worth dick if you're just reinventing the wheel?
>buh muh token costs!!
Irrelevant. If price to run the grid increases because token become more valuable, then whole economic incentive to migrate to your web infrastructure falls apart.

62284265
>>62275591
did they ever get dapps?

62284324
>>62284091
>buy signal

under $20k btc

62284332
>>62284265
i don't think so, who wants to learn haskell

62287225
lol, lmao, etc

62287288
>still worth more than xlm
Tragic

62290899
>>62287225
Just lmfao

62290974
>>62284332
Haskell is arguably one of the worst programming languages ever invented.

62291005
>>62287225
wth is rain
>>62275591
saw this happening when they didn't integrate with Chainlink

62291011
>>62290974
plutus core isn't strictly speaking haskell
it's lambda- calculus
but that's not the reason cardano struggles to have a virtual machine

anyway, cardano can't really "collapse" because its TVL is already very low, there isn't really any money moving, so the security budget of its staking BFT is enough to order everything correctly
but I doubt it will significantly have any real volume either

62291214
https://x.com/IOHK_Charles/status/2060082349963120728

item.title: Question for oldfags about vidya prices: — 48 replies — 9.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Question for oldfags about vidya prices:

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-05-30 00:53 UTC

OP

i looked into the price of SNES games as well as master system back in the days:

-average snes game sold for 50$ in the early 9às, inflation adjusted that's 120$ today
Some sold higher than that : donkey kong came for 70$, so 150$ today

-30 to 60$ for sega master system, but it was the late 80s so like 130$ today.

Why are gooners crying about the price of their vidyas? Things used to be way more expensive for a 2 d platformer that would give you max 100 hours of play if not half. Also today's collector price for most of those games is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new back in the days, so still no profit after holding for 30+ years kek

Early replies (first 3)

62283131
>>62283119
There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.

62283133
>>62283119
Oldfag here, they’re crying because all these games were $1 at your local flea market in 2005, AND THEY DIDN’T EVEN WANT TO BUY. It’s even worse than missing bitcoin because it was right under their nose, they saw, they knew, and they shrugged it off.

62283135
>>62283131
snes games were cheap like 10 + years ago. Master system is still very cheap, but nobody gives a shit. I guess rn the money is in xbox / game cube games

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286216
>>62286204
Street Fighter II for SNES was amazing despite not being arcade where some nignog could chimp out after getting assblasted by Blanka

62286230
>>62285855
>rent games? so lame.
Less so than selling your own games.

>did anyone actually do that?
Yes and it was amazing, especially when renting those esoteric JP games and playing them with an adapter. It was like temporarily unlocking a new world, you were inviting all your friends to explore these games to the fullest for the weekend.

Of course a lonely consoomer from 2026 can’t get it.

62286236
>>62286216
It was very enjoyable indeed, but still not as good as the arcade version.

62286393
>>62283119
My most sacred memory is when my mom picked me up from 1st grade in 1987 and as we are driving home she told me that she got me a new video game where you can SAVE the game and start from there when you come back to it. WOW.

62286541
>>62283119
When I got my first PC in 1997 at 14 I rarely bought games after that. I had consoles but I didn't have money to buy games and my parents wouldn't just buy me any game I wanted whenever.

62286565
>>62283119
They're bitching just to bitch. There's also like 1000 ways to emulate those games and play them like the originals or better, so really they're retardedly nostalgic to have THE cartridge and then mad a bunch of other people are too

62286669
>>62283133
Nobody is talking about used video games. They're crying because modern consoles are approaching a thousand bucks and moden games are heading towards 100. But anon correctly noted that back in the day, it was $50-60 for a new NES cartridge or a new computer game on three 360kb diskettes, and at the time minimum wage was something like $4 an hour. Adjusted for inflation, a game like Super Mario that you could beat in an hour, or Kings Quest that lasts you a couple of hours max, cost $161.

62287647
>>62283131
>There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.
This
I bought a movie rental stores stock for peanuts when they were going broke in 2012, walked in and offered 2000$ cash for the lot (about 5000 DVDs and blu rays and 2500 PS1, PS2 and 360 games). It was a niche movie buff DVD and game store. Have a pretty complete movie and game library and enjoy it a lot to this day. I am randy marsh.

62287668
>>62286669
>Nobody is talking about used video games.
OP was literally talking about "today's collector price for most of those games" but okay

62287673
>>62287668
both can come into discussion, but the idea was that game used to be even more expensive than they are today yet people bitch about their free world 10k hours lifetime game selling for half what a super nintendo game used to sell

62287727
>>62283119
I don't know what a SNES is (Nintendo consoles are for faggots), but I used to buy pirated PS1/PS2 games for $5 from some shady dude back in the day. Even back then, only retards would pay $50 for a video game. I see times haven't changed...

62288182
>>62287668
Yes.
>today's colllector price is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new
He's still talking about how prices today are still lower when accounting for inflation, even for used collector games, compared to the price for games back in the day.
And he's right. A nintendo entertainment system with one game (Mario/Duck Hunt) adjusted for inflation cost $450.
A New 386 16 with 1mb of ram and a 20mb hard drive with 16 color graphics and midi sound card cost $8500, adjusted for inflation that's $25,000.
People bitching about prices for games these days really have no idea. Their idea of what things cost back in the day is skewed by a combination television where the latest technology was placed "in the common home" as a deliberate advertisement and a long run of (relatively) dramatically discounted prices from the late 90s until the late 10s.
Even today their expectations are ridiculous: "It shouldn't cost as much as when you had physical media because you can just download it!", like servers and connections that can provide fast 85gb video game downloads to millions of people around the world are not expensive themselves.

62288229
>>62283119
People's real wages were also higher.

62288318
>>62283119
I played that game on that console when I was a kid.

62288477
>>62287673
>>62288182
This is true, video games are cheaper in real terms and even in nominal terms today. Even the "expensive" Switch games are not pricier than Street Fighter II in 1993. But the essential things of life are much more expensive (rent, food), which makes video games comparatively just as hard (or harder?) to afford. Hence the complaining.

The crybabies will also complain when a game is free though:
>waaaah I don’t get enough pulls to get my waifu in Genshin Impact, even though I spent $0 in five years of playing daily

62288623
>>62288477
>>62288318
>>62288182
i had a friend at school who had a dedicated playroom room with every fucking super nes game as well as mega drive. Spoiled brat, parents probably were LOADED. That made me diamond at the time

62288647
>>62283119
vidya felt way more expensive and special. buying a new vidya was an important life decision for many, you had to study that shit, read game magazine articles about vidya to weigh which to invest in, you had to save up money, potentially sell other vidya to frens for a bit of cash (like actual paper bills or even actual metal coins). back then when a neighbor kid got a new vidya it was an event and kids would gather to check it out or ask to borrow it someday. i invested all my money in vidya and bought a new one per month. don't get me started on buying a new console, that felt similar to buying a new fridge, something you needed to buy for basic survival because the old one was just not doing it anymore.

62288698
>>62288623
>>62288647
Kek, back then you knew exactly how many games you had, and could recite the list of all your games like it’s a poem.

Each game was considered a rather big purchase and the games felt more "precious" in general. Also true with the PS1-era games.

62288801
>>62283119
muh inflation is a cope shit tier non-argument.
snes and mega drive games were also printed on very expensive cartridges and game makers also had to pay a lot to retail.
now most sales are digital, so there is literally zero actual scarcity or logistics involved and the market is way bigger than it ever was.

basically kys

62289331
>>62288229
>People's real wages were also higher.
No, they really weren't. The people making this claim base it on the price of Gold in 1970 and extrapolate it from there. (or they compare themselves to characters on 70s and 80s television) The price of Gold was artificially suppressed for 30 years by Bretton Woods. The median salary in 1987 was $30,000 a year. If you made above 60,000 you were upper middle class. Pic related was a middle class home and cost 120k new. People also like to pretend that there weren't oil shocks and 20% inflation in the 70s and 80s wiping out that majority of boomers' savings, or that a mortgage for such a home didn't come with a 16% interest rate.

62289351
>>62288801
>there is literally zero actual scarcity or logistics involved
Servers and high speed connections are not zero logistics.
You don't even put two and two together. In the heady days of the wild west internet of the early 2000s, bandwidth was cheap, but it was also slow. People don't realize that because they demanded HD (and later 4K) streaming that infrastructure costs have skyrocketed so much that only the richest corporations can afford to provide that kind of service. You wonder why there's so much consolidation of media, why the major corporations have bought up so many smaller studios, why social media and video streaming is reduced to a handful of winners, it's because the bandwidth necessary to transmit the large amounts of data you're demanding is huge, and Bandwidth ain't actually cheap at all.

62289407
>>62283119
games used to actually be good, and they provided you with hundreds of hours of entertainment. not the dog shit coming out today that you beat in 8 hrs and then never play again

62289601
>>62283119
This game took me 4 days to beat as a kid so definitely not 100 hours.

62289722
>>62289351
>Servers and high speed connections are not zero logistics.
retarded.

62290884
>>62283119
In 1982-83 atari 2600 games were routine 30 bucks, that's over 100 inflation adjusted....

62290896
>>62283119
>inflation adjusted that's 120$ today
wages arent inflation adjusted.

62291071
>>62286669
>Kings Quest that lasts you a couple of hours max
Kings Quest actually had some difficult puzzles. Nobody beat it in a couple hours without cheats and/or walkthroughs

62291136
>>62283119
Daily good were relatively cheaper, so it evened out or felt less bad

62291154
>muh inflation
>>62289351
The developer would have gotten 7 dollars off of a 70 dollar 4MB SNES cartridge. Seven.
Today they get around 20. Adjust that for inflation and they've beaten inflation. About 15 bucks if you take cpi is what the cut in the snes era was worth.

Everything about digital distribution is cheaper than sticking roms into plastic boxes and shipping them internationally for them to sit in warehouses and later retail stores. And it could be even cheaper Steam's monopoly keeps things artificially high.

62291182
>>62291154
>Steam
I've been buying off GOG lately, unfortunately they don't have everything.

item.title: ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly — 34 replies — 8.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-05-30 02:04 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62285243
>>62285128
crazy the amount of time people spend fudding a random alt coin.
it happens so much, the amount of energy that's spent on it buy a group of individuals, or a bot? either way, it's very interesting from the outside
made me start to think the linkies are onto something..
to anyone else on the outside, like anyone here from pmg or smg? how does this look? pretty crazy to me..
anyway the constant fud against link made me think i should secure a suicide bag of it, and now i just buy 1 link everytime someone posts a fud thread for free, in their own spare time.. which is crazy to me
so must be trying to hide something

62285245
quick, make more threads!

62285257
>>62285128
so true, king

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285535
>>62285243
>i just buy 1 link everytime someone posts a fud thread for free, in their own spare time.. which is crazy to me
STFU nigga you aint doin that dont lie fees would rape u

62285592
>>62285535
like 1-2 cent a buy?

62285749
>>62285128
>chainlink staking mechanism colorized

62285799
>>62285128
Ikyk

62285888
>>62285243
Link monkeys are Lolcows. DUH.

62287261
>>62285888
anymore then bbby or gamestop guys? yet you don't see 20 threads fudding or shitting on them everyday?
it's pretty weird..
i have less then 500 link btw, at average of 7-8, i literally don't care if it moons or not, just one of many "if the schizos are right' small bags
plus we all learned link triggers you transgenic, homos so much, hence my continued dca, despite knowing nothing about chainlink or dtcc or orcale (thought that was a different company)

62287574
>>62285243
From my perspective, all crypto is a scam unless its called FARTCOIN

62287615
>>62287261

>I'm DCAing something I don't even understand that is dieing because it "owns the transgenic"

lmao what a fucking retard

62287648
>>62287615
retards retard
retards win

62287707
>>62285243
nah it's just a natural reaction to the link shill spam that's been going on since 2017, but you know this since you are a shill

62287709
>>62287615
>dieing
I haven't owned Link since 2020 but I might just buy some after reading your post

62287712
Very effective link thread. I'm even tempted to buy it, even though I don't do crypto.

62287714
>>62285535
What is a percentage

62287716
>>62287712
>"Hello fellow huwhite saars, by vishnu i think we should all redeem our apple gift cards and buy link tokens saars, berry good coin saar"

62287729
>>62287261
Both of them keep it to a general. Linkfaggots, in contrast, spammed /biz/ for years.

62287741
>>62285243
you're a mentally retarded cuckold LARPing as a non LINK holder. Chainlink is trading at a lower price than 6 years ago

62288220
>>62287729
>>62287707
>spam up the board as a response to link spam
genuis
>>62287741
i have a little link because of the constant schizo war between fudders and shills, just makes me think i should have a little in case
I never even told anyone to buy link in this thread, link isn't even 1 percent of my port.

62288375
>>62285882
underrated (i don't even use this board but it sums up 4chan pretty nicely)

62288389
>>62285128
>AI slop
Yep. This is /biz/ alright.

62288421
>>62285128

62288446
>>62285243

wait so you do individual $9 buys of an altcoin to "own" random dudes online?

how are the fees on those buys champ

grim

62288528
>>62288365
This is me but i somehow ended up with a career, a wife and a house.

62288547
>>62288446
no fees bro, not that hard to figure out. I'm not using market order hot swaps or some shit. I have fee free trading on a CEX

62288565
>>62285128

62291294
>>62285882
sucks to your austhism

62291316
>>62288565
Are they friends?

item.title: how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking? — 38 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking?

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-05-30 01:14 UTC

OP

France has seen an explosion in crypto kidnapping. This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances. There also was a data leak from some kind of private investment company after a hack.
People with balances as low as 2 btc have been targetted and successfully forced to give up all their assets under threats. Some people living abroad have had their relatives kidnapped.

How do you avoid this shit? all the regulations on crypto have been an absolute disaster, the only end goal is to steal your wealth via taxation and state control. This totally ruins the whole crypto thing. There is no point in holding large amounts of cryptos if it puts a target on your back and on your familly and you can't sleep well at night.
Now many anons might think this is a french thing but eventually more leaks will happen and nobody will be safe. there will be lists with your name and address and all the purchase you did in the last years, even maybe your depo address that criminals will be able to track and see what most likely happened with your balance.

One simple mitigation measure would be to use many exchanges, many adresses, as to make it appear you hold less, but this is also an annoying process that require strict management and doesn't guarantee additional leaks at the state level if they group all your trading infos.
You should stop using any KYC, stop paying taxes on government that don't care at all about your safety, and go full down the crypto ecosystem giving up off ramps as much as you can.

Early replies (first 3)

62287742
Generic India hate thread disguised as something else.

62287753
>>62287731
>France
That's what you get for living in Africa.

62287759
>>62287753
while i can't disagree, the problem remains. if you think living in the usa or germany makes you safe from this issue you're delulu. Usa is known for its violent crime just wait til a list of easy 1 Mil++ robberies on individual citizens start going around and see what happens

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287859
>>62287850
>>62287821
>>62287831

While your answers are completely selfish and retarded, i took a visit to rubic and rubic actually allows cross chain swaps between bitcoin and many others stables so that's one way you can do crypto to crypto trade without KYC, and sell high to rebuy lower without every retard gov agency being aware of it.
I'm there are a lot of solutions out there.

62287875
>>62287731
the gov home address leaks are the insane part of this coz you cant really prevent that without not reporting (which is kinda impossible if you ever want to spend a decent amount)

only way to prevent i guess is having a holiday home and just pointing to that

62287892
>>62287821
Database leaks happen all the time. Its just a matter of time before a KYC exchange gets breached and your info gets leaked

62287918
>>62287731
>vidrel
Poor wagie, it sucks that everything is filmed now. He can't forget this even if I bet he wants to.

62287930
>>62287731
>This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances
All by design

62287931
>>62287731
>I have a lot of money in crypto/cash/gold
>Omg why am I a target for robbery?!
What did you expect, faggot? Life like this. It's the way it is

62287985
>>62287731
1. lie
2. do not ever disclose your balance
3. have 2 homes in case

62288016
>>62287931
very forward thinking anon.
Difference is with a bank account it's much more anonymous and you can't just get robbed in a wrench key attack.

62288126
>>62287892
>KYC exchange
retard. THE FUCKING STATE IS MAKING YOU DO IT

62288135
>>62287985
>GOY ID
Kek has spoken.

62288136
>>62287759
>easy robberies
Nigga I will kill anyone who comes around trying to rob me. I am armed and insane.

62288150
>>62288136
Yeah, imagine giving up your seed phrase to some angry African because it has a gun that it barely knows how to use.

62288560
>>62287731
that video brought me so much joy, thank you.

62289395
>>62288560
i couldnt imagine a stupider gimmick than a rollerblade setup in a high-end, indoor restaurant, where alot of soup, stew and curry-style foodstuffs are served.

It makes sense in an outdoors setting like in america, where you're serving lightweight packaged food, and its probably more efficient overall to use rollerblades. But this is pajeet-tarded.

62289432
>>62289395
Rollerblade service should only be done by red headed tomboys with short hair and freckles with a small chest, a bubble butt, and wide hips.

62289484
>>62288150
>>62288136
how about they kidnap your elderly parents or any other important relative and start cutting their fingers live if you don't give the seed? which is their new modus operandi and they did it in france to a guy who is living in dubai but his father lives here

62289530
>>62287731
1. Dont live in france
1. Dont live in europe
2. Have multiple dummy wallet and load it with small amounts of stables and some shitcoins
3. Dont live in france

62289586
>>62289432
Too bad here is your skinny gay jeet. Fuck you.

t. Jew that doesn’t deal in the real world

62289590
>>62287742
do not capitalize the word "india"

62289690
>>62289586
>t. Jew
we smell it. choke on a baby dick.

62289723
>>62287731
Lmao.

62289923
>>62289432
>>62289590
based takes

>>62289484
sounds like in that particular case, the guy was realllllly retarded and publicly shitposted his wealth on social media. If he lives in dubai, he doesn't need to declare shit as income retard.

62289932
>>62289923
he was just running a crypto related business in dubai. I'm telling you it's a matter of time before this happens more or less everywhere where death penatly isn't the norm for this shit

62289939
>>62289923
you think it's still a matter of bragging, we are well past that point. Criminals gangs have lists of people and their assets either from hacks, or government officials selling those infos. they hire street level retards thugs to do the job from another country. Somehow the criminals found out he was the ceo of some business in dubai, probably not very hard to find.

62289944
>>62289932
so then it had nothing to do with Franceistan's laws, and it was just a case of an exposed person getting exploited.

Police need to do alot more to curb organised crime, but Anarcho-tyranny is the order of the day in the west, commies are just plain evil.

62290407
>>62287731
>own firearms
>shoot home invaders
gee that wasn't so hard now was it?

62291194
>>62287731
>declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances

There's the problem. Never declare shit and go through monero or other privacy coins to protect your privacy.

62291200
>>62287731
this doesn't happen where I live because we often execute criminals while they're engaged in the crime.

62291231
>>62287731
If you live as a rich man in a poor society, you are a target.
If you live as middle class in a middle class society, your community can protect you.

This was discovered during Americans golden age, and has now been forgotten.
I look forward to the horrors of war visiting the small minded selfish cunts who currently run the world.

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 59 replies — 7.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-05-29 21:47 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275952
>>62275842
Again dude I just finished my first out of three years of law school, so I don’t know what the actual practice looks like at all.

62276043
>>62275952
I'm just ribbing you buddy, a little white collar banter if you will

62277022
>>62275816
>170 LSAT

Acceptable.

>Mid 20s Law School

Acceptable.

>3.5 1L GPA

Acceptable.

>I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

It’s over. You are cooked. BIG oof. Thanks for playing.

62277039
>>62277022
Why is it over? And why didn’t you address my penis size?

62277080
>>62277039
If you are not interning at an AMLAW 50 firm your 1L summer then you literally need to kill yourself.

[spoiler]My posts are intended to satirize the rampant one-upsmanship in the legal profession. I don’t actually care about your genitalia you dumb retard. It is a metaphor.[/spoiler]

62278084
>>62277080
Well don’t scare me like that anon. You freaked me out since some of the people in my class do have Big Law internships and it’s kinda tough not to feel jealous sometimes with how much they’re getting paid.

62278202
>>62278084
Funposting aside, (You) should be (mildly) freaked out because spending your 1L summer split between volunteering for a professor and a one (1) month stint with a (non-federal) jjudge is concerning. Especially if the stats (You) quoted me earlier are true.

(You) are underachieving.

62278255
>>62278202
I don't know what to tell you. I applied for a billion 1L summer positions and even got a lot of interviews but none of them panned out. It would have been nice to get something more prestigious looking but I'm just glad I finally got something lined up. i have heard also that as long as you do do something legal related 1L summer then firms don't really care what it was, is that not true?

I'm not actually aiming for Big Law or anything, I'm mostly interested in environmental and real estate law and potentially being a prosecutor. I dunno I've only taken the basic classes and it feels weird this expectation to know for certain what you want to do before you've learned anything about the field.

62278315
>>62278255
I appreciate your sincerity, and I will stop frogposting.

Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid. I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms. These days, Big Law internships are reserved for black lesbians (unironically). You’re doing fine.

Godspeed.

62278366
>>62278315
>Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid
I am getting paid for the RA job though.
>I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms
This might be self-evident as a 4chan poster but I absolutely hate networking, really suck at it, and would like to avoid it as much as possible. Is it really that essential to getting a good job? Do I just have to force myself to do it? I heard the 2L summer hiring process was supposed to be way chiller and less insane.

And thanks for your advice by the way!

62278395
>>62278366
Networking is somewhat required for any career path unfortunately. My condolences anon

62280144
>>62278395

62280365
>>62278366
You will miss out of some of the best opportunities in this profession if you don’t network, unfortunately. I only got my current job because one of my professors was willing to refer me to someone on the inside.

62280758
Are there any good legal loopholes for evading debt collection? I'm writing a story, and there's a chapter where the MC scrambles to hide his assets from the chapter 7 trustee.

62281712
>>62280758
I know several but will tell you none

62282639
>>62280758
>I'm writing a story
I dunno if I believe you there anon

62286326
>>62272812
Truebit was hacked in January you think a lawsuit has a chance?

62286469
>>62272812
How much are jews over-represented in law?

Can I get in with a science degree and mediocre GPA (3.5)? If i can't get a science job with my MS then thinking of pivoting into patent law.

62286482
what are the reputations of people who go into different types of law? like are torts people regarded as ambulance chasers? people who go into real estate considered retards? etc

62286539
how do I sue companies for discrimination during the interview process?

62286591
>>62273041
>the irish people have done nothing for humanity, and neither will you. You are niggers compared to Russians

62286708
>>62273090
What were your classmates like? Like how nursing is single moms and programming is white sexual deviants and engineering students are just a bunch of Aryan chads.

62286727
>>62272812
Kys lawfag

62286755
>>62286326
Probably not
>>62286469
Definitely overrepresented but I dunno how much.
>>62286482
I definitely know personal injury lawyers are seen as being kind of skeavy, the civil rights and public interest stereotype is they're very wealthy and privileged with their parents paying for everything but still act condescending towards everyone trying to get paid. I don't really know stereotypes for the rest.
>>62286539
You basically can't unless you have a smoking gun like emails between each other saying they were discriminating or they accidentally sent you a voicemail joking about that guy they just discriminated against
>>62286708
Probably 60% women, and 95% white other than one asian girl. There were a few different cliques but the only really shared trait was alcoholism and being nerds. Some are the friendly type of nerds and some the smug type of nerds. Like there was one guy who would go to TA office hours and when someone would ask the TA a question he would very quickly butt in and try to answer the question for the TA, then at the end ask the TA "is that basically right?" pissed people off. Most people were just normal though.
>>62286727
Why come?

62287528
I know you said it's only for /biz/ questions, but I'm really curious: do you think Jigsaw could ever be prosecuted? This is something we're regularly discussing on >>>/tv/.

62287539
>>62272812
Is gooning illegal if she doesn't notice?

62289826
>>62287528
The guy who kidnaps people and puts them in death traps? What would even be the argument for him not being guilty of murder? To be guilty of murder and found to be the cause of someone’s death, you have to be the but-for cause of their death (but for the defendant doing X, person would not have died) and the proximate cause (basically means them dying was foreseeable from defendants actions). But-for Jigsaw putting them in the traps, they would not have died and their deaths were extremely foreseeable
>>62287539
Yes it’s illegal, but you can’t be charged with anything you’re the only one who knows about

62290013
>>62287539
bit of gooning on the side dont hurt anybody

62290634
how pretigious do you place your job in society? is there any job you think is more prestigious?

62290695
oh and by "prestigious" i mean being respected and earning big bucks, not like firefighter-prestige

item.title: I am a 37 year old incel who has been in crypto since 2017 and hasn't made it, and I am all in HYPE. — 13 replies — 6.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am a 37 year old incel who has been in crypto since 2017 and hasn't made it, and I am all in HYPE.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-30 03:58 UTC

OP

I am a 37 year old incel who has been in crypto since 2017 and hasn't made it, and I am all in HYPE.

Early replies (first 3)

62290966
>>62290940
what was your entry price?

62290988
>>62290966
Check your dubs and you'll know my entry price

62290989
I swung trade shitcoins in 2020 and got 'fully invested' in 2021, didn't make it, swore I'd buy 5 bitcoins between 2022 and 2024 and make it (I thought BTC was going to 220k a coin), didn't make it and now I have exactly 3% of my poorfolio in Hype. I will not make it this time either.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62291028
>>62290940
>>62290989
Holy mother of top signals

62291040
>>62291028
Don't say that, let him find out by himself why one doesn't go all in during bear market in a billion marketcap coin

62291050
I'm going to foretell exactly what is going to happen, screencap this. Hype has a couple of weeks left of fuel, I expect at max until SpaceX IPO to keep pumping, after that, it will CRASH and crash hard with Bitcoin. Now the price is soemthing difficult to predict, but it will top somewhere between $70-100, so there's not much room left, so at best 80% upside.
This guy won't believe me and he will probably hold it thinking it will go to $200 or even more delusional and thinking this can reach in the short term $1000 and it won't. He will baghold it until next bull and as with any oter DeXes coins, it usually never recovers t's top. Now it HyperEVM does well as a L1, it will potentially reach new ATHs.

I'm not fudding, I plan buying some, but not until BTC bottoms.

62291321
>>62291050
actually my target is $1200, imagine thinking a $50B marketcap is delusional.

62291323
$100 waiting room

62291359
>>62291040
I'm only 3% in though

62291371
>>62291321
$1200 is the same as 320B marketcap if there aren't any unlocks or burns. And btw there's only 1/4 of total supply circulating. If you believe it will be the top 2 crypto next bull and dethrone ETH by 3-4x it's top marketcap this bull (basically the same as a trillion marketcap), then go for it, but be realistic, otherwise you will join the linkmarines.

62291430
>>62291371
Unlike link HYPE is actually centered around token value. Most platform fees go to buybacks and burns

62291446
>>62291371
what does eth do that justifies a valuation of 6000 or more, its projected top in 2028? The value proposal for eth was being burned as gas for shitcoins but people don't care about shitcoins now, people want to trade stocks 24/7 while high as fuck at 4 am on a saturday because President Trump tweeted he's about to nuke chinese semiconductors or something to keep american dominance and that will make nvidia soar on monday and you want to be in that trade before it happens. Hype is used as the gas to make that happen.

And if we assume the next bullrun takes bitcoin near 200k then the overall crypto marketcap should rise accordingly, so there will be money moving in Hype's direction.

Plus, cheetoman tweeted about bringing perp dexes back to america.

62291479
Anyone buying hype at over 70 weekly RSI.
